memoryArtifacts.headline: Trim-Castle- in County Meath Ireland was built by Hugh de Lacy and his son Walter de Lacy
memoryArtifacts.description:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trim_Castle
Trim Castle (Irish: Caisleán Bhaile Atha Troim) is a Norman castle on the south bank of the River Boyne in Trim, County Meath, Ireland. With an area of 30,000 m², it is the largest Norman castle in Ireland. Over a period of 30 years, it was built by Hugh de Lacy and his son Walter as the caput of the Lordship of Meath.

popularStory.headline: Biography
popularStory.description: Hugh de Lacy, Lord of Meath, 4th Baron Lacy (born before 1135, died 25 July 1186), was an Anglo-Norman landowner and royal office-holder. He had substantial land holdings in Herefordshire and Shropshi …

zh.lifeSummary: Hugh de Lacy Lord of Meath是Gilbert de Lacy的兒子，於1135年之前出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾。他於大約1181年與Princess Rose Ní Conchobair在愛爾蘭庫迪 米亞斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1186年7月25日在愛爾蘭庫迪 勞瓦斯度洛，埋葬在愛爾蘭度布林。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lacy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lassy in Calvados, named from a Gaulish personal name Lascius (of uncertain origin and meaning) + the locative suffix -acum.

variants.name: Lasee
meanings: Probably an altered form of English or Irish   Lacy  .

variants.name: Lacey
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Lacy  .

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.description: Arrival of Normans at Baginbun in Co. Wexford thus started 800 year struggle between English and Irish.

en.lifeSummary: Hugh de Lacy Lord of Meath was born before 1135, in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Gilbert de Lacy. He married Princess Rose Ní Conchobair about 1181, in County Meath, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 25 July 1186, in Durrow, County Laois, Ireland, and was buried in Dublin, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Hugh de Lacy Lord of Meath est né avant 1135, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Gilbert de Lacy. Il a épousé Princess Rose Ní Conchobair environ 1181, à County Meath, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 25 juillet 1186, à Durrow, County Laois, Irlande, et a été enterré à Dublin, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Hugh de Lacy Lord of Meath wurde vor 1135 in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Gilbert de Lacy geboren. Er hat um 1181 in County Meath, Irland Princess Rose Ní Conchobair geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 25. Juli 1186 in Durrow, County Laois, Irland und wurde in Dublin, Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Hugh de Lacy Lord of Meath è nato il prima del 1135 nel Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Gilbert de Lacy. Lui sposato Princess Rose Ní Conchobair circa 1181, nel County Meath, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 25 luglio 1186, nel Durrow, County Laois, Irlanda, e fu sepolto a Dublin, Irlanda.

ja.Ewias, Herefordshire, England: イギリスハーエフォードシュアイアエウィアス
ja.lifeSummary: Hugh de Lacy Lord of Meathは1135年以前にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアでGilbert de Lacyの息子として生まれた。約1181年にアイルランドコウントイ メアスでPrincess Rose Ní Conchobairと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1186年7月25日にアイルランドコウントイ ラオイスダローで死去し，アイルランドダブリンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh de Lacy Lord of Meath(은)는 1135년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르에서 Gilbert de Lacy의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1181년 아일랜드 카운티 미스에서 Princess Rose Ní Conchobair(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1186년 7월 25일 아일랜드 카운티 라이스 뒤로에서 사망했으며 아일랜드 드블린에 묻혔다.

pt.from March 1172 to 25 July 1186: de março de 1172 a 25 de julho de 1186
pt.lifeSummary: Hugh de Lacy Lord of Meath nasceu antes 1135, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de Gilbert de Lacy. Ele casou-se com Princess Rose Ní Conchobair aproximadamente 1181, em County Meath, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 25 de julho de 1186, em Durrow, County Laois, Irlanda, e foi sepultado em Dublin, Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Hugh de Lacy Lord of Meath родился перед 1135 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали Gilbert de Lacy. Он женился на женщине по имени Princess Rose Ní Conchobair около 1181 г. в местности коунти мит, Ирландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 25 июля 1186 г. в местности дурроу, коунти лаоис, Ирландия и был похоронен в местности дублинь, Ирландия.
